name: Jordan
summary: Day 103 / 2026-06-10 (Wednesday): **NODE CREATED — JORDAN ENTERS THE GRAPH AS THE FIRST NON-GULF ARAB US ALLY STRUCK BY IRAN. Early Wednesday the IRGC launched a long-range missile strike on Azraq airbase in Jordan (alongside drones/missiles on the US Fifth Fleet HQ in Bahrain and Ali Al Salem airbase in Kuwait) — the FIRST Iranian strike on Jordanian soil this war, widening the target geography beyond the Gulf.** The IRGC CLAIMED 21 US targets attacked and 4 destroyed including an F-35 hangar at Azraq; against that, **Jordan's military said it intercepted ALL FIVE missiles**, with only shrapnel fall and 'no human injuries or material damage' (CONFIRMED interception — Jordanian military statement, corroborated by US-official 'intercepted nearly all' framing). Jordan's communiqué was studiedly technical (five missiles, intercepted, shrapnel, no damage) — Amman minimising to avoid obligating itself to a response. This is the third iteration of the established Iranian pattern (June 5-6 Kuwait/Bahrain barrage; the CENTCOM-denied Fifth Fleet claim; now this): **spectacular claims, intercepted reality, zero casualties** — performative-deterrence strikes calibrated to demonstrate reach without killing Americans or allied personnel; the genuinely new element is the GEOGRAPHY (a Levant, non-Gulf US ally). The Arab League (Aboul Gheit) condemned the 'brutal' attacks and declared 'full solidarity' with Jordan, Bahrain and Kuwait; no expulsions or diplomatic downgrades followed — words-not-actions continues. Jordan hosts US forces at Azraq (and is a long-standing US security ally and peace-treaty partner of Israel), placing it on the seam of the conflict's geography; it is also, like the Gulf states, a financial beneficiary of the proposed Bessent reconstruction mechanism. **NODE-CREATION JUSTIFICATION:** persistent entity (sovereign state); appears in 2+ independent sources (Jordanian military statement; Al Jazeera; CNN/Reuters/NBC pool; Tasnim's claim channel); causally connected to existing nodes iran (struck it) and united-states (hosts US forces at Azraq, US ally). **TRIGGER DECISIONS (June 10):** NO trigger to active — the strike was intercepted and zero-casualty (CONFIRMED interception; IRGC damage assertions CLAIMED and denied). Added one watching trigger on a confirmed-lethal Iranian strike on Jordanian soil (the threshold that would convert Jordan from a struck-but-intercepting bystander into an active belligerent and test the US/Israel mutual-defence posture).

active_triggers.condition: Confirmed lethal Iranian strike on Jordanian soil (US/allied-personnel or Jordanian-civilian deaths), verified by 2+ operational sources
active_triggers.note: The June 10 Azraq strike was intercepted (all 5 missiles, per Jordan's military) with zero casualties — performative-deterrence, not a lethal escalation. A CONFIRMED lethal strike would break the zero-casualty calibration on a NEW front (a Levant US ally + Israel peace-treaty partner), pulling Jordan toward active belligerency and testing the US mutual-defence posture. Stays watching until a confirmed lethal event crosses the threshold.

top_edges.context: 2026-06-10: IRGC fired long-range missiles at Azraq airbase — first Iranian strike on Jordanian soil this war. Jordan's military intercepted all 5 (shrapnel only, no injuries/damage); IRGC's F-35-hangar-destroyed claim DENIED. Performative-deterrence; widens target geography to the Levant.

2026-06-10: Jordan hosts US forces at Azraq (long-standing US security ally + Israel peace-treaty partner). Iran's strike on Azraq targeted US presence on Jordanian soil; US reported zero injuries/damage. Jordan minimised publicly to avoid obligating a response.

recent_signals.headline: Hit for a SECOND consecutive day -- ballistic missiles at the Al-Azraq base; Jordan said it intercepted 20 missiles with 'no casualties or material damage.' Confined itself to interception statements -- no expulsion, no escalation. The iran->jordan edge bumped on the second confirmed strike.

**JORDAN STRUCK BY IRAN FOR THE FIRST TIME — AZRAQ AIRBASE HIT BY LONG-RANGE MISSILES, ALL FIVE INTERCEPTED, ZERO CASUALTIES.** Early Wednesday the IRGC fired long-range missiles at Azraq airbase in Jordan (alongside Bahrain's Fifth Fleet HQ and Kuwait's Ali Al Salem) — the first Iranian strike on Jordanian soil this war, widening the target geography beyond the Gulf to a Levant US ally. Jordan's military said it intercepted all five missiles (shrapnel only, 'no human injuries or material damage'); the IRGC claimed it destroyed an F-35 hangar at Azraq — DENIED. Third iteration of the spectacular-claims/intercepted/zero-casualty pattern. Arab League condemned the 'brutal' attacks and declared 'full solidarity'; no expulsions or downgrades — words, not actions. (CONFIRMED interception — Jordanian military statement; Al Jazeera; CNN/Reuters/NBC pool. CLAIMED damage — Tasnim/IRGC, denied.) (intel A5)
sources: US official via Reuters/CNN/NBC pool (no injuries/damage)
recent_signals.verification: CONFIRMED (strike occurred + interceptions) / CLAIMED (IRGC damage assertions, denied)
